title: Lirianthe annamensis
intro: Lirianthe annamensis is a flowering plant species within the family Apocynaceae. It is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and improving overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Lirianthe annamensis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as tea, tinctures, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and skin problems.
taxonomy: Lirianthe annamensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is native to Laos and Vietnam. The classification of Lirianthe annamensis includes it being in the genus Lirianthe, which is part of the family Apocynaceae. The Apocynaceae family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as dogbane or oleander family. Lirianthe annamensis is considered a rare and endangered species due to its limited range and habitat.
